card.intuition: An automated tool for improving the quality of web pages, providing scores across Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, and SEO.
card.analogy: A school report card for your website: it gives you an overall GPA (the 0-100 score) and then breaks it down by subject (Performance, SEO, etc.), telling you exactly where you need to study more.
when_to_use: Auditing a website's performance during development
Identifying accessibility failures (missing alt tags, low contrast)
Ensuring the site follows modern web best practices
gotchas: A 100/100 score in Lighthouse doesn't guarantee a perfect user experience; it's a 'lab' measurement, not 'field' data from real users
Performance score is a weighted average; LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) usually has the highest weight

steps.label: Audit
steps.annotation: Lighthouse loads the page and collects metrics via Chrome DevTools.

steps.label: Performance
steps.annotation: Core Web Vitals measured — weighted into performance score (0-100).

steps.label: Accessibility
steps.annotation: Checks colour contrast, ARIA roles, focus management.

steps.label: Best Practices
steps.annotation: Checks HTTPS, deprecated APIs, console errors.

steps.label: SEO
steps.annotation: Checks meta descriptions, viewport, structured data.

exercise.prompt: Identify the four main categories of a Lighthouse report.
exercise.starter_code:
categories = [
    "1. ___________ (Speed and optimization)",
    "2. _____________ (Usability for impaired users)",
    "3. Best _________ (Modern standards)",
    "4. ___ (Search Visibility)"
]
exercise.test_code:
assert "Performance" in categories[0]
assert "Accessibility" in categories[1]
print("PASS")
